### REQUEST ###
```
POST https://api2.cursor.sh:443/aiserver.v1.RepositoryService/FastUpdateFileV2 HTTP/1.1
Accept-Encoding: gzip
Authorization: Bearer REDACTED
Connect-Protocol-Version: 1
Connect-Timeout-Ms: 180000
Connection: close
Content-Encoding: gzip
Content-Type: application/proto
Host: api2.cursor.sh
Traceparent: REDACTED
User-Agent: connect-es/1.6.1
X-Amzn-Trace-Id: REDACTED
X-Cursor-Checksum: REDACTED
X-Cursor-Client-Arch: x64
X-Cursor-Client-Device-Type: desktop
X-Cursor-Client-Os: win32
X-Cursor-Client-Type: ide
X-Cursor-Client-Version: 2.6.11
X-Cursor-Config-Version: REDACTED
X-Cursor-Timezone: REDACTED
X-Ghost-Mode: true
X-New-Onboarding-Completed: false
X-Request-Id: REDACTED
X-Session-Id: REDACTED
```

{
  "1:LEN": {
    "1:I64": 4843024894782521597
  },
  "2:LEN": "REDACTED",
  "6:VARINT": 4,
  "7:LEN": [
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI, Response\n\nfrom mini_celery.api.routes_health import router as health_router\nfrom mini_celery.api.routes_jobs import router as jobs_router\nfrom mini_celery.broker.redis_streams import RedisStreamsBroker\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\nfrom mini_celery.db import Database, get_db\nfrom mini_celery.logging import setup_logging\nfrom mini_celery.metrics import metrics_content_type, metrics_payload\nfrom mini_celery.repository import Repository\n\n\ndef create_app(\n    *,\n    settings: Settings | None = None,\n    db: Database | None = None,\n    broker: RedisStreamsBroker | None = None,\n) -> FastAPI:\n    effective_settings = settings or get_settings()\n    effective_db = db or get_db()\n\n    @asynccontextmanager\n    async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):\n        setup_logging(effective_settings.log_level)\n        effective_db.init()\n        effective_broker = broker or RedisStreamsBroker(settings=effective_settings)\n        for queue in effective_settings.queues:\n            await effective_broker.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\n\n        app.state.settings = effective_settings\n        app.state.db = effective_db\n        app.state.repo = Repository(effective_db.SessionLocal)\n        app.state.broker = effective_broker\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            await effective_broker.close()\n\n    app = FastAPI(title=effective_settings.app_name, lifespan=lifespan)\n    app.include_router(jobs_router)\n    app.include_router(health_router)\n\n    @app.get(\"/metrics\")\n    async def metrics() -> Response:\n        return Response(content=metrics_payload(), media_type=metrics_content_type())\n\n    return app\n\n\napp = create_app()\r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "c48a56ac97a91fba0b37e8f707c2b8c6056774d7e3a754e2c6bbed8086c6a639",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport yaml\nfrom croniter import croniter\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field\n\nfrom mini_celery.broker.redis_streams import RedisStreamsBroker\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\nfrom mini_celery.db import Database, get_db\nfrom mini_celery.logging import setup_logging\nfrom mini_celery.metrics import jobs_enqueued_total, queue_depth, scheduler_enqueues_total\nfrom mini_celery.repository import Repository\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ScheduleConfig(BaseModel):\n    id: str\n    cron: str\n    queue: str = \"default\"\n    type: str\n    payload: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)\n    max_attempts: int = Field(default=3, ge=1)\n    enabled: bool = True\n\n\nclass ScheduleFile(BaseModel):\n    schedules: list[ScheduleConfig] = Field(default_factory=list)\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass SchedulerDependencies:\n    settings: Settings\n    db: Database\n    broker: RedisStreamsBroker\n\n\ndef _to_utc(dt: datetime) -> datetime:\n    if dt.tzinfo is None:\n        return dt.replace(tzinfo=UTC)\n    return dt.astimezone(UTC)\n\n\ndef load_schedules(path: str) -> list[ScheduleConfig]:\n    file_path = Path(path)\n    if not file_path.exists():\n        logger.warning(\"schedule config not found\", extra={\"path\": str(file_path)})\n        return []\n\n    raw = file_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    data: dict[str, Any]\n    if file_path.suffix.lower() == \".json\":\n        data = json.loads(raw)\n    else:\n        data = yaml.safe_load(raw) or {}\n\n    parsed = ScheduleFile.model_validate(data)\n    return parsed.schedules\n\n\nclass Scheduler:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        repo: Repository,\n        broker: RedisStreamsBroker,\n        settings: Settings,\n        schedules: list[ScheduleConfig],\n        clock: callable | None = None,\n    ):\n        self.repo = repo\n        self.broker = broker\n        self.settings = settings\n        self.schedules = schedules\n        self.clock = clock or (lambda: datetime.now(UTC))\n        self.stop_event = asyncio.Event()\n        self.last_scan_at = self.clock() - timedelta(seconds=max(settings.scheduler_poll_interval_sec, 1.0))\n\n    async def run(self) -> None:\n        logger.info(\"scheduler started\", extra={\"schedules\": len(self.schedules)})\n        while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                await self.run_once()\n            except Exception:\n                logger.exception(\"scheduler loop failed\")\n            await asyncio.sleep(self.settings.scheduler_poll_interval_sec)\n\n    async def run_once(self, now: datetime | None = None) -> None:\n        current = _to_utc(now or self.clock())\n        await self.dispatch_recurring(current)\n        await self.dispatch_due_jobs(current)\n        for queue in self.settings.queues:\n            depth = await self.broker.queue_depth(queue)\n            queue_depth.labels(queue=queue).set(depth)\n        self.last_scan_at = current\n\n    async def dispatch_due_jobs(self, now: datetime) -> None:\n        due = self.repo.get_due_jobs(now, limit=200)\n        for job in due:\n            fields = {\n                \"job_id\": job.id,\n                \"type\": job.type,\n                \"queue\": job.queue,\n                \"enqueued_at\": _to_utc(now).isoformat(),\n                \"dispatch_token\": str(job.version + 1),\n            }\n            stream_id = await self.broker.enqueue(job.queue, fields)\n            if self.repo.mark_enqueued(job.id, stream_id):\n                jobs_enqueued_total.labels(type=job.type, queue=job.queue).inc()\n                logger.info(\n                    \"scheduler enqueued due job\",\n                    extra={\n                        \"job_id\": job.id,\n                        \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                        \"state\": job.state.value,\n                        \"queue\": job.queue,\n                    },\n                )\n\n    async def dispatch_recurring(self, now: datetime) -> None:\n        for schedule in self.schedules:\n            if not schedule.enabled:\n                continue\n\n            for planned in self._due_ticks(schedule.cron, self.last_scan_at, now):\n                created = self.repo.create_scheduled_job_if_new(\n                    schedule_id=schedule.id,\n                    planned_time=planned,\n                    queue=schedule.queue,\n                    type=schedule.type,\n                    payload=schedule.payload,\n                    max_attempts=schedule.max_attempts,\n                )\n                if created:\n                    scheduler_enqueues_total.labels(schedule_id=schedule.id).inc()\n                    logger.info(\n                        \"scheduler created recurring job\",\n                        extra={\n                            \"job_id\": created.id,\n                            \"schedule_id\": schedule.id,\n                            \"state\": created.state.value,\n                        },\n                    )\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _due_ticks(cron_expr: str, start: datetime, end: datetime) -> list[datetime]:\n        start_utc = _to_utc(start)\n        end_utc = _to_utc(end)\n        if end_utc <= start_utc:\n            return []\n\n        ticks: list[datetime] = []\n        iterator = croniter(cron_expr, start_utc - timedelta(seconds=1))\n        while True:\n            candidate = _to_utc(iterator.get_next(datetime))\n            if candidate > end_utc:\n                break\n            ticks.append(candidate)\n        return ticks\n\n    def stop(self) -> None:\n        self.stop_event.set()\n\n\nasync def _run_service(settings: Settings) -> None:\n    setup_logging(settings.log_level)\n    db = get_db()\n    db.init()\n\n    repo = Repository(db.SessionLocal)\n    broker = RedisStreamsBroker(settings=settings)\n    for queue in settings.queues:\n        await broker.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\n\n    schedules = load_schedules(settings.scheduler_config_path)\n    scheduler = Scheduler(repo=repo, broker=broker, settings=settings, schedules=schedules)\n\n    try:\n        await scheduler.run()\n    finally:\n        await broker.close()\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"mini-celery scheduler\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--config\", dest=\"config\", default=None)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    settings = get_settings()\n    if args.config:\n        settings.scheduler_config_path = args.config\n    asyncio.run(_run_service(settings))\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "30752d19b302373ad3c6889b3286e8aa916f565c0517ec51ddc0a343926b1712",

    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        "2:LEN": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },

          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        "2:LEN": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport asyncio\nimport contextlib\nimport logging\nimport random\nimport socket\nimport time\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom mini_celery.broker.redis_streams import RedisStreamsBroker\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\nfrom mini_celery.db import get_db\nfrom mini_celery.logging import setup_logging\nfrom mini_celery.metrics import (\n    job_duration_seconds,\n    jobs_finished_total,\n    jobs_started_total,\n    queue_depth,\n    worker_reclaims_total,\n)\nfrom mini_celery.models import JobState\nfrom mini_celery.repository import ClaimOutcome, Repository\nfrom mini_celery.tasks.base import JobCancelledError, TaskContext, TaskExecutionError\nfrom mini_celery.tasks.types import build_task_registry\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass Worker:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        repo: Repository,\n        broker: RedisStreamsBroker,\n        settings: Settings,\n        consumer: str,\n        rng: random.Random | None = None,\n    ):\n        self.repo = repo\n        self.broker = broker\n        self.settings = settings\n        self.consumer = consumer\n        self.registry = build_task_registry(settings)\n        self.stop_event = asyncio.Event()\n        self.semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(settings.worker_concurrency)\n        self.active_tasks: set[asyncio.Task[None]] = set()\n        self.rng = rng or random.Random()\n\n    async def run(self) -> None:\n        logger.info(\n            \"worker started\",\n            extra={\n                \"consumer\": self.consumer,\n                \"queues\": self.settings.queues,\n                \"concurrency\": self.settings.worker_concurrency,\n            },\n        )\n        for queue in self.settings.queues:\n            await self.broker.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\n\n        reclaim_task = asyncio.create_task(self._reclaim_loop())\n\n        try:\n            while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n                processed_any = False\n                for queue in self.settings.queues:\n                    messages = await self.broker.read_group(\n                        queue=queue,\n                        consumer=self.consumer,\n                        count=max(1, self.settings.worker_concurrency),\n                        block_ms=500,\n                    )\n                    if messages:\n                        processed_any = True\n                    for stream_id, fields in messages:\n                        await self._schedule_handle_message(queue, stream_id, fields, reclaimed=False)\n\n                if not processed_any:\n                    await asyncio.sleep(0.05)\n        finally:\n            self.stop_event.set()\n            reclaim_task.cancel()\n            with contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError):\n                await reclaim_task\n\n            if self.active_tasks:\n                await asyncio.gather(*self.active_tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n\n    async def _reclaim_loop(self) -> None:\n        min_idle_ms = self.settings.visibility_timeout_sec * 1000\n        while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                for queue in self.settings.queues:\n                    claimed = await self.broker.claim_stale(\n                        queue=queue,\n                        consumer=self.consumer,\n                        min_idle_ms=min_idle_ms,\n                        count=100,\n                    )\n                    if claimed:\n                        worker_reclaims_total.inc(len(claimed))\n                    for stream_id, fields in claimed:\n                        await self._schedule_handle_message(queue, stream_id, fields, reclaimed=True)\n                    depth = await self.broker.queue_depth(queue)\n                    queue_depth.labels(queue=queue).set(depth)\n            except Exception:\n                logger.exception(\"reclaim loop failed\", extra={\"consumer\": self.consumer})\n\n            await asyncio.sleep(self.settings.reclaim_interval_sec)\n\n    async def _schedule_handle_message(\n        self,\n        queue: str,\n        stream_id: str,\n        fields: dict[str, str],\n        reclaimed: bool,\n    ) -> None:\n        await self.semaphore.acquire()\n        task = asyncio.create_task(self._handle_message(queue, stream_id, fields, reclaimed))\n        self.active_tasks.add(task)\n\n        def _done(_: asyncio.Task[None]) -> None:\n            self.semaphore.release()\n            self.active_tasks.discard(task)\n\n        task.add_done_callback(_done)\n\n    async def _handle_message(\n        self,\n        queue: str,\n        stream_id: str,\n        fields: dict[str, str],\n        reclaimed: bool,\n    ) -> None:\n        job_id = fields.get(\"job_id\")\n        task_type = fields.get(\"type\", \"unknown\")\n        if not job_id:\n            await self.broker.ack(queue, stream_id)\n            return\n\n        claim: ClaimOutcome = self.repo.claim_for_execution(\n            job_id=job_id,\n            stream_id=stream_id,\n            consumer=self.consumer,\n            visibility_timeout_sec=self.settings.visibility_timeout_sec,\n            now=datetime.now(UTC),\n        )\n\n        if claim.action == \"ack\":\n            await self.broker.ack(queue, stream_id)\n            return\n        if claim.action == \"skip\" or claim.job is None:\n            return\n\n        running = claim.job\n        started_monotonic = time.monotonic()\n        jobs_started_total.labels(type=running.type).inc()\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"job execution started\",\n            extra={\n                \"job_id\": job_id,\n                \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                \"consumer\": self.consumer,\n                \"attempt\": running.attempt,\n                \"state\": JobState.RUNNING.value,\n                \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n            },\n        )\n\n        context = TaskContext(\n            job_id=job_id,\n            attempt=running.attempt,\n            should_cancel=lambda: self.repo.is_cancel_requested(job_id),\n        )\n\n        should_ack = False\n        finished_state: JobState | None = None\n\n        try:\n            result = await self.registry.execute(running.type, running.payload, context)\n            finished_state = self.repo.finalize_success(\n                job_id=job_id,\n                result=result,\n                stream_id=stream_id,\n            )\n            should_ack = True\n        except JobCancelledError:\n            finished_state = self.repo.finalize_canceled(job_id=job_id, stream_id=stream_id)\n            should_ack = True\n        except TaskExecutionError as exc:\n            policy = self.registry.backoff_for(running.type)\n            delay_sec = policy.compute_delay(running.attempt, rng=self.rng)\n            outcome = self.repo.finalize_failure(\n                job_id=job_id,\n                stream_id=stream_id,\n                error=str(exc),\n                retryable=exc.retryable,\n                delay_sec=delay_sec,\n            )\n            finished_state = outcome.state\n            should_ack = True\n            if outcome.state == JobState.DEAD:\n                await self.broker.publish_dlq(\n                    {\n                        \"job_id\": job_id,\n                        \"type\": running.type,\n                        \"attempt\": str(running.attempt),\n                        \"error\": str(exc),\n                        \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                    }\n                )\n        except Exception as exc:\n            policy = self.registry.backoff_for(running.type)\n            delay_sec = policy.compute_delay(running.attempt, rng=self.rng)\n            outcome = self.repo.finalize_failure(\n                job_id=job_id,\n                stream_id=stream_id,\n                error=f\"unexpected error: {exc}\",\n                retryable=True,\n                delay_sec=delay_sec,\n            )\n            finished_state = outcome.state\n            should_ack = True\n            if outcome.state == JobState.DEAD:\n                await self.broker.publish_dlq(\n                    {\n                        \"job_id\": job_id,\n                        \"type\": running.type,\n                        \"attempt\": str(running.attempt),\n                        \"error\": f\"unexpected error: {exc}\",\n                        \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                    }\n                )\n            logger.exception(\n                \"job execution failed unexpectedly\",\n                extra={\n                    \"job_id\": job_id,\n                    \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                    \"consumer\": self.consumer,\n                    \"attempt\": running.attempt,\n                },\n            )\n\n        duration = max(0.0, time.monotonic() - started_monotonic)\n        job_duration_seconds.labels(type=running.type).observe(duration)\n        if finished_state is not None:\n            jobs_finished_total.labels(type=running.type, state=finished_state.value).inc()\n\n        if should_ack:\n            await self.broker.ack(queue, stream_id)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"job execution finished\",\n            extra={\n                \"job_id\": job_id,\n                \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                \"consumer\": self.consumer,\n                \"attempt\": running.attempt,\n                \"state\": finished_state.value if finished_state else \"UNKNOWN\",\n            },\n        )\n\n    def stop(self) -> None:\n        self.stop_event.set()\n\n\nasync def _run_service(settings: Settings, consumer: str) -> None:\n    setup_logging(settings.log_level)\n    db = get_db()\n    db.init()\n\n    broker = RedisStreamsBroker(settings=settings)\n    repo = Repository(db.SessionLocal)\n    worker = Worker(repo=repo, broker=broker, settings=settings, consumer=consumer)\n\n    try:\n        await worker.run()\n    finally:\n        await broker.close()\n\n\ndef _default_consumer_name() -> str:\n    return f\"worker-{socket.gethostname()}-{int(time.time())}\"\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"mini-celery worker\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--consumer\", default=_default_consumer_name())\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    settings = get_settings()\n    asyncio.run(_run_service(settings, consumer=args.consumer))\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "0cc9cd3e340d580de774e802d5d0da08c0d71e19ea812fe5bfc7f7051d59ed92",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    }
  ]
}
```

### RESPONSE ###
```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Grpc-Status\, Grpc-Message\, Grpc-Status-Details-Bin\, Content-Encoding\, Connect-Content-Encoding\, traceparent\, backend-traceparent\, x-amzn-trace-id\, x-request-id
Content-Type: application/proto
Date: Thu\, 05 Mar 2026 04:08:09 GMT
Vary: Origin
```

```
{
  "1:VARINT": 1
}
```

### SIGNATURE ###
```
http:host is localhost
http:method is POST
http:queryparams is -NONE-
http:requestBodyJSON is {"1:LEN": {"1:I64": 4843024894782521597}, "2:LEN": "REDACTED", "6:VARINT": 4, "7:LEN": [{"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI, Response\n\nfrom mini_celery.api.routes_health import router as health_router\nfrom mini_celery.api.routes_jobs import router as jobs_router\nfrom mini_celery.broker.redis_streams import RedisStreamsBroker\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\nfrom mini_celery.db import Database, get_db\nfrom mini_celery.logging import setup_logging\nfrom mini_celery.metrics import metrics_content_type, metrics_payload\nfrom mini_celery.repository import Repository\n\n\ndef create_app(\n    *,\n    settings: Settings | None = None,\n    db: Database | None = None,\n    broker: RedisStreamsBroker | None = None,\n) -> FastAPI:\n    effective_settings = settings or get_settings()\n    effective_db = db or get_db()\n\n    @asynccontextmanager\n    async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):\n        setup_logging(effective_settings.log_level)\n        effective_db.init()\n        effective_broker = broker or RedisStreamsBroker(settings=effective_settings)\n        for queue in effective_settings.queues:\n            await effective_broker.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\n\n        app.state.settings = effective_settings\n        app.state.db = effective_db\n        app.state.repo = Repository(effective_db.SessionLocal)\n        app.state.broker = effective_broker\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            await effective_broker.close()\n\n    app = FastAPI(title=effective_settings.app_name, lifespan=lifespan)\n    app.include_router(jobs_router)\n    app.include_router(health_router)\n\n    @app.get(\"/metrics\")\n    async def metrics() -> Response:\n        return Response(content=metrics_payload(), media_type=metrics_content_type())\n\n    return app\n\n\napp = create_app()\r\n"}, "2:LEN": "c48a56ac97a91fba0b37e8f707c2b8c6056774d7e3a754e2c6bbed8086c6a639", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport yaml\nfrom croniter import croniter\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field\n\nfrom mini_celery.broker.redis_streams import RedisStreamsBroker\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\nfrom mini_celery.db import Database, get_db\nfrom mini_celery.logging import setup_logging\nfrom mini_celery.metrics import jobs_enqueued_total, queue_depth, scheduler_enqueues_total\nfrom mini_celery.repository import Repository\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ScheduleConfig(BaseModel):\n    id: str\n    cron: str\n    queue: str = \"default\"\n    type: str\n    payload: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)\n    max_attempts: int = Field(default=3, ge=1)\n    enabled: bool = True\n\n\nclass ScheduleFile(BaseModel):\n    schedules: list[ScheduleConfig] = Field(default_factory=list)\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass SchedulerDependencies:\n    settings: Settings\n    db: Database\n    broker: RedisStreamsBroker\n\n\ndef _to_utc(dt: datetime) -> datetime:\n    if dt.tzinfo is None:\n        return dt.replace(tzinfo=UTC)\n    return dt.astimezone(UTC)\n\n\ndef load_schedules(path: str) -> list[ScheduleConfig]:\n    file_path = Path(path)\n    if not file_path.exists():\n        logger.warning(\"schedule config not found\", extra={\"path\": str(file_path)})\n        return []\n\n    raw = file_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    data: dict[str, Any]\n    if file_path.suffix.lower() == \".json\":\n        data = json.loads(raw)\n    else:\n        data = yaml.safe_load(raw) or {}\n\n    parsed = ScheduleFile.model_validate(data)\n    return parsed.schedules\n\n\nclass Scheduler:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        repo: Repository,\n        broker: RedisStreamsBroker,\n        settings: Settings,\n        schedules: list[ScheduleConfig],\n        clock: callable | None = None,\n    ):\n        self.repo = repo\n        self.broker = broker\n        self.settings = settings\n        self.schedules = schedules\n        self.clock = clock or (lambda: datetime.now(UTC))\n        self.stop_event = asyncio.Event()\n        self.last_scan_at = self.clock() - timedelta(seconds=max(settings.scheduler_poll_interval_sec, 1.0))\n\n    async def run(self) -> None:\n        logger.info(\"scheduler started\", extra={\"schedules\": len(self.schedules)})\n        while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                await self.run_once()\n            except Exception:\n                logger.exception(\"scheduler loop failed\")\n            await asyncio.sleep(self.settings.scheduler_poll_interval_sec)\n\n    async def run_once(self, now: datetime | None = None) -> None:\n        current = _to_utc(now or self.clock())\n        await self.dispatch_recurring(current)\n        await self.dispatch_due_jobs(current)\n        for queue in self.settings.queues:\n            depth = await self.broker.queue_depth(queue)\n            queue_depth.labels(queue=queue).set(depth)\n        self.last_scan_at = current\n\n    async def dispatch_due_jobs(self, now: datetime) -> None:\n        due = self.repo.get_due_jobs(now, limit=200)\n        for job in due:\n            fields = {\n                \"job_id\": job.id,\n                \"type\": job.type,\n                \"queue\": job.queue,\n                \"enqueued_at\": _to_utc(now).isoformat(),\n                \"dispatch_token\": str(job.version + 1),\n            }\n            stream_id = await self.broker.enqueue(job.queue, fields)\n            if self.repo.mark_enqueued(job.id, stream_id):\n                jobs_enqueued_total.labels(type=job.type, queue=job.queue).inc()\n                logger.info(\n                    \"scheduler enqueued due job\",\n                    extra={\n                        \"job_id\": job.id,\n                        \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                        \"state\": job.state.value,\n                        \"queue\": job.queue,\n                    },\n                )\n\n    async def dispatch_recurring(self, now: datetime) -> None:\n        for schedule in self.schedules:\n            if not schedule.enabled:\n                continue\n\n            for planned in self._due_ticks(schedule.cron, self.last_scan_at, now):\n                created = self.repo.create_scheduled_job_if_new(\n                    schedule_id=schedule.id,\n                    planned_time=planned,\n                    queue=schedule.queue,\n                    type=schedule.type,\n                    payload=schedule.payload,\n                    max_attempts=schedule.max_attempts,\n                )\n                if created:\n                    scheduler_enqueues_total.labels(schedule_id=schedule.id).inc()\n                    logger.info(\n                        \"scheduler created recurring job\",\n                        extra={\n                            \"job_id\": created.id,\n                            \"schedule_id\": schedule.id,\n                            \"state\": created.state.value,\n                        },\n                    )\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _due_ticks(cron_expr: str, start: datetime, end: datetime) -> list[datetime]:\n        start_utc = _to_utc(start)\n        end_utc = _to_utc(end)\n        if end_utc <= start_utc:\n            return []\n\n        ticks: list[datetime] = []\n        iterator = croniter(cron_expr, start_utc - timedelta(seconds=1))\n        while True:\n            candidate = _to_utc(iterator.get_next(datetime))\n            if candidate > end_utc:\n                break\n            ticks.append(candidate)\n        return ticks\n\n    def stop(self) -> None:\n        self.stop_event.set()\n\n\nasync def _run_service(settings: Settings) -> None:\n    setup_logging(settings.log_level)\n    db = get_db()\n    db.init()\n\n    repo = Repository(db.SessionLocal)\n    broker = RedisStreamsBroker(settings=settings)\n    for queue in settings.queues:\n        await broker.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\n\n    schedules = load_schedules(settings.scheduler_config_path)\n    scheduler = Scheduler(repo=repo, broker=broker, settings=settings, schedules=schedules)\n\n    try:\n        await scheduler.run()\n    finally:\n        await broker.close()\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"mini-celery scheduler\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--config\", dest=\"config\", default=None)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    settings = get_settings()\n    if args.config:\n        settings.scheduler_config_path = args.config\n    asyncio.run(_run_service(settings))\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\r\n"}, "2:LEN": "30752d19b302373ad3c6889b3286e8aa916f565c0517ec51ddc0a343926b1712", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "2:LEN": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "2:LEN": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport asyncio\nimport contextlib\nimport logging\nimport random\nimport socket\nimport time\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom mini_celery.broker.redis_streams import RedisStreamsBroker\nfrom mini_celery.config import Settings, get_settings\nfrom mini_celery.db import get_db\nfrom mini_celery.logging import setup_logging\nfrom mini_celery.metrics import (\n    job_duration_seconds,\n    jobs_finished_total,\n    jobs_started_total,\n    queue_depth,\n    worker_reclaims_total,\n)\nfrom mini_celery.models import JobState\nfrom mini_celery.repository import ClaimOutcome, Repository\nfrom mini_celery.tasks.base import JobCancelledError, TaskContext, TaskExecutionError\nfrom mini_celery.tasks.types import build_task_registry\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass Worker:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        repo: Repository,\n        broker: RedisStreamsBroker,\n        settings: Settings,\n        consumer: str,\n        rng: random.Random | None = None,\n    ):\n        self.repo = repo\n        self.broker = broker\n        self.settings = settings\n        self.consumer = consumer\n        self.registry = build_task_registry(settings)\n        self.stop_event = asyncio.Event()\n        self.semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(settings.worker_concurrency)\n        self.active_tasks: set[asyncio.Task[None]] = set()\n        self.rng = rng or random.Random()\n\n    async def run(self) -> None:\n        logger.info(\n            \"worker started\",\n            extra={\n                \"consumer\": self.consumer,\n                \"queues\": self.settings.queues,\n                \"concurrency\": self.settings.worker_concurrency,\n            },\n        )\n        for queue in self.settings.queues:\n            await self.broker.ensure_consumer_group(queue)\n\n        reclaim_task = asyncio.create_task(self._reclaim_loop())\n\n        try:\n            while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n                processed_any = False\n                for queue in self.settings.queues:\n                    messages = await self.broker.read_group(\n                        queue=queue,\n                        consumer=self.consumer,\n                        count=max(1, self.settings.worker_concurrency),\n                        block_ms=500,\n                    )\n                    if messages:\n                        processed_any = True\n                    for stream_id, fields in messages:\n                        await self._schedule_handle_message(queue, stream_id, fields, reclaimed=False)\n\n                if not processed_any:\n                    await asyncio.sleep(0.05)\n        finally:\n            self.stop_event.set()\n            reclaim_task.cancel()\n            with contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError):\n                await reclaim_task\n\n            if self.active_tasks:\n                await asyncio.gather(*self.active_tasks, return_exceptions=True)\n\n    async def _reclaim_loop(self) -> None:\n        min_idle_ms = self.settings.visibility_timeout_sec * 1000\n        while not self.stop_event.is_set():\n            try:\n                for queue in self.settings.queues:\n                    claimed = await self.broker.claim_stale(\n                        queue=queue,\n                        consumer=self.consumer,\n                        min_idle_ms=min_idle_ms,\n                        count=100,\n                    )\n                    if claimed:\n                        worker_reclaims_total.inc(len(claimed))\n                    for stream_id, fields in claimed:\n                        await self._schedule_handle_message(queue, stream_id, fields, reclaimed=True)\n                    depth = await self.broker.queue_depth(queue)\n                    queue_depth.labels(queue=queue).set(depth)\n            except Exception:\n                logger.exception(\"reclaim loop failed\", extra={\"consumer\": self.consumer})\n\n            await asyncio.sleep(self.settings.reclaim_interval_sec)\n\n    async def _schedule_handle_message(\n        self,\n        queue: str,\n        stream_id: str,\n        fields: dict[str, str],\n        reclaimed: bool,\n    ) -> None:\n        await self.semaphore.acquire()\n        task = asyncio.create_task(self._handle_message(queue, stream_id, fields, reclaimed))\n        self.active_tasks.add(task)\n\n        def _done(_: asyncio.Task[None]) -> None:\n            self.semaphore.release()\n            self.active_tasks.discard(task)\n\n        task.add_done_callback(_done)\n\n    async def _handle_message(\n        self,\n        queue: str,\n        stream_id: str,\n        fields: dict[str, str],\n        reclaimed: bool,\n    ) -> None:\n        job_id = fields.get(\"job_id\")\n        task_type = fields.get(\"type\", \"unknown\")\n        if not job_id:\n            await self.broker.ack(queue, stream_id)\n            return\n\n        claim: ClaimOutcome = self.repo.claim_for_execution(\n            job_id=job_id,\n            stream_id=stream_id,\n            consumer=self.consumer,\n            visibility_timeout_sec=self.settings.visibility_timeout_sec,\n            now=datetime.now(UTC),\n        )\n\n        if claim.action == \"ack\":\n            await self.broker.ack(queue, stream_id)\n            return\n        if claim.action == \"skip\" or claim.job is None:\n            return\n\n        running = claim.job\n        started_monotonic = time.monotonic()\n        jobs_started_total.labels(type=running.type).inc()\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"job execution started\",\n            extra={\n                \"job_id\": job_id,\n                \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                \"consumer\": self.consumer,\n                \"attempt\": running.attempt,\n                \"state\": JobState.RUNNING.value,\n                \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n            },\n        )\n\n        context = TaskContext(\n            job_id=job_id,\n            attempt=running.attempt,\n            should_cancel=lambda: self.repo.is_cancel_requested(job_id),\n        )\n\n        should_ack = False\n        finished_state: JobState | None = None\n\n        try:\n            result = await self.registry.execute(running.type, running.payload, context)\n            finished_state = self.repo.finalize_success(\n                job_id=job_id,\n                result=result,\n                stream_id=stream_id,\n            )\n            should_ack = True\n        except JobCancelledError:\n            finished_state = self.repo.finalize_canceled(job_id=job_id, stream_id=stream_id)\n            should_ack = True\n        except TaskExecutionError as exc:\n            policy = self.registry.backoff_for(running.type)\n            delay_sec = policy.compute_delay(running.attempt, rng=self.rng)\n            outcome = self.repo.finalize_failure(\n                job_id=job_id,\n                stream_id=stream_id,\n                error=str(exc),\n                retryable=exc.retryable,\n                delay_sec=delay_sec,\n            )\n            finished_state = outcome.state\n            should_ack = True\n            if outcome.state == JobState.DEAD:\n                await self.broker.publish_dlq(\n                    {\n                        \"job_id\": job_id,\n                        \"type\": running.type,\n                        \"attempt\": str(running.attempt),\n                        \"error\": str(exc),\n                        \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                    }\n                )\n        except Exception as exc:\n            policy = self.registry.backoff_for(running.type)\n            delay_sec = policy.compute_delay(running.attempt, rng=self.rng)\n            outcome = self.repo.finalize_failure(\n                job_id=job_id,\n                stream_id=stream_id,\n                error=f\"unexpected error: {exc}\",\n                retryable=True,\n                delay_sec=delay_sec,\n            )\n            finished_state = outcome.state\n            should_ack = True\n            if outcome.state == JobState.DEAD:\n                await self.broker.publish_dlq(\n                    {\n                        \"job_id\": job_id,\n                        \"type\": running.type,\n                        \"attempt\": str(running.attempt),\n                        \"error\": f\"unexpected error: {exc}\",\n                        \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                    }\n                )\n            logger.exception(\n                \"job execution failed unexpectedly\",\n                extra={\n                    \"job_id\": job_id,\n                    \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                    \"consumer\": self.consumer,\n                    \"attempt\": running.attempt,\n                },\n            )\n\n        duration = max(0.0, time.monotonic() - started_monotonic)\n        job_duration_seconds.labels(type=running.type).observe(duration)\n        if finished_state is not None:\n            jobs_finished_total.labels(type=running.type, state=finished_state.value).inc()\n\n        if should_ack:\n            await self.broker.ack(queue, stream_id)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"job execution finished\",\n            extra={\n                \"job_id\": job_id,\n                \"stream_id\": stream_id,\n                \"consumer\": self.consumer,\n                \"attempt\": running.attempt,\n                \"state\": finished_state.value if finished_state else \"UNKNOWN\",\n            },\n        )\n\n    def stop(self) -> None:\n        self.stop_event.set()\n\n\nasync def _run_service(settings: Settings, consumer: str) -> None:\n    setup_logging(settings.log_level)\n    db = get_db()\n    db.init()\n\n    broker = RedisStreamsBroker(settings=settings)\n    repo = Repository(db.SessionLocal)\n    worker = Worker(repo=repo, broker=broker, settings=settings, consumer=consumer)\n\n    try:\n        await worker.run()\n    finally:\n        await broker.close()\n\n\ndef _default_consumer_name() -> str:\n    return f\"worker-{socket.gethostname()}-{int(time.time())}\"\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"mini-celery worker\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--consumer\", default=_default_consumer_name())\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    settings = get_settings()\n    asyncio.run(_run_service(settings, consumer=args.consumer))\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\r\n"}, "2:LEN": "0cc9cd3e340d580de774e802d5d0da08c0d71e19ea812fe5bfc7f7051d59ed92", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}]}
http:url is /aiserver.v1.RepositoryService/FastUpdateFileV2
```

uuid: REDACTED

duration: 1125ms
tags: captureMode=proxy, decoded=true, proxyProtocol=tcp:http, proxyType=dual, proxyVersion=v2.5.182, reverseProxyHost=localhost, reverseProxyPort=8080, sequence=1357, source=goproxy

GMT"],"Vary":["Origin"]},"bodyBase64":"REDACTED"}},"signature":{"http:host":"bG9jYWxob3N0","http:method":"UE9TVA==","http:queryparams":"","http:requestBodyJSON":"","http:url":"L2Fpc2VydmVyLnYxLlJlcG9zaXRvcnlTZXJ2aWNlL0Zhc3RVcGRhdGVGaWxlVjI="},"netinfo":{"id":"REDACTED","startTime":"2026-03-05T04:08:09.134455600Z","downstream":{"established":"2026-03-05T04:08:08.943557800Z","ipAddress":"REDACTED","port":10092,"bytesSent":"6296"},"upstream":{"established":"2026-03-05T04:08:09.024966Z","ipAddress":"REDACTED","port":443,"hostname":"api2.cursor.sh","bytesSent":"375"}}}
